word_list.word: there is nothing new under the sun
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈʌθɪŋ nˈuː ˌʌndɚ ðə sˈʌn

senses.definition: used to suggest that everything that happens or exists has already been experienced or observed in some form before
used to imply that a new person, who is not yet set in their ways, is more efficient and effective in implementing change than someone who has been in the position for a long time
used to encourage individuals to recognize and appreciate the fact that every historical period or generation has a unique perspective and approach to understanding the world

word_list.word: from the sweetest wine, the tartest vinegar
word_list.pronunciation: fɹʌmðə swˈiːɾəst wˈaɪn ðə tˈɑːɹɾəst vˈɪnᵻɡɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that the greatest successes or pleasures in life can sometimes lead to the greatest failures or disappointments

word_list.word: there are no birds in last year's nest
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹˌɑːɹ nˈoʊ bˈɜːdz ɪn lˈæst jˈɪɹz nˈɛst

senses.definition: used to suggest that conditions and circumstances are always changing, and that relying on past successes or achievements is not a guarantee of future success

word_list.word: times change and we with time
word_list.phonetic_text: taɪmz ʧeɪnʤ ænd wi wɪð taɪm
word_list.phonetic_am_text: taimz cheinj ānd vi vidh taim
word_list.pronunciation: tˈaɪmz tʃˈeɪndʒ ænd wiː wɪð tˈaɪm

senses.definition: used to say that as time passes, people may adapt and change to new circumstances and situations, including societal norms and standards

word_list.word: variety is the spice of life
word_list.pronunciation: vɚɹˈaɪəɾi ɪz ðə spˈaɪs ʌv lˈaɪf

senses.definition: used to imply that it is important to seek out and embrace new experiences, activities, and pleasures in life to keep things interesting and enjoyable

examples.x: He loved to travel and explore new places, believing that variety is the spice of life.

word_list.word: you cannot put new wine (in|into) old bottles
word_list.pronunciation: juː kænˈɑːt pˌʊt nˈuː wˈaɪn ɪn ˌɪntʊ ˈoʊld bˈɑːɾəlz

senses.definition: used to imply that it is very difficult or impossible to change something that is already set in its ways

word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi flˈoʊ hɐz hæθ ɪts ˈɛb

senses.definition: used to suggest that every negative period will eventually be followed by a positive period, and vice versa, suggesting that life is a constant cycle of ups and downs
